heading: PERFORMANCE OF TEACHING SERVICES BY EMPLOYEE IN LIEU OF PAYMENT OF SCHOOL TAXES ON PROPERTY OF BUSINESS ENTITY
operative_text: The governing body of a school district by resolution may authorize a corporation or other business entity to permit a qualified individual employed by the business entity to perform teaching services in a high school or a junior high school for the school district in lieu of paying taxes imposed by the district on property owned by the business entity.

defined_terms.definition_excerpt:
An individual is qualified to perform teaching services for a school district under this section only if the individual holds a baccalaureate or more advanced degree in a field related to the course to be taught and:
(1) is certified as a classroom teacher under Subchapter B, Chapter 21, Education Code; or
(2) obtains a school district teaching permit under Section 21.055, Education Code.

_note: Statute authorizes school district to grant property tax credits (max $500/course/semester; $1,000 for full-year-credit courses) to business entities whose employees teach in district high/junior high schools. Employee is not a district employee and receives no district benefits.
